entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary: Demurrage introduces significant complexities into protocols such as the Lightning network and is incompatible with existing implementations. This concept, although theoretically feasible to be incorporated via a soft fork where transactions must include an output with the demurrage-taxed amount directed towards future mining fees, is impractical for actual implementation. The economic characteristics of demurrage mirror those of inflation, with both essentially acting as a tax on savings. The primary distinction lies in the method of tax application. For more detailed insights on this topic, one can refer to the works of Peter Todd, accessible through his [website](https://petertodd.org).
